title: ficus parietalis var. tabing
intro: Ficus parietalis var. tabing, also known as the "Tabing Ficus," is a variety of the Ficus genus, which comprises over 800 species of trees and shrubs. Traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, the Tabing Ficus has been employed to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of the plant have been attributed to its leaves, which are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. Tabing Ficus can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, with some herbalists suggesting it can help alleviate symptoms of fever, improve digestion, and boost the immune system.
